title: Search Quality Validation Test Harness Pattern
summary: A systematic search quality validation harness that runs test queries against a search index and scores results as pass/partial/fail based on expected file matches and source type coverage. Each test case defines a query, expected files, and must-include source types. The harness runs FTS5 first, falls back to multi-term LIKE for missing results, and reports per-query status with matched files and tags. Aggregate summary shows total/pass/partial/fail counts.
why_reusable: Any search system needs quality regression testing. This harness pattern can be adapted to any search backend (Elasticsearch, PostgreSQL FTS, Meilisearch, etc.) by swapping the search function. The pass/partial/fail classification and expected-files validation logic is backend-agnostic.
adapter_target: Generic search quality test suite — extract the validate_one + summary logic into a test framework that accepts a search function and test cases as input.
implementation_notes: Test case format: query, language, intent, expected_files, must_include_source_types
Three-tier result: pass (files+types hit), partial (some hit or results exist), fail (no results)
FTS5 with bm25 ordering first; multi-term LIKE fallback fills missing results
Tokenization: regex word extraction, min length 2, stopword filtering (and/with/the/for)
Report includes per-query matched files, matched tags (top 40), and hit counts
Exit code reflects test result: 0 if no failures, 1 if any fail
tests_required: Unit test: pass classification when expected files and types are found
Unit test: partial classification when some but not all criteria met
Unit test: fail classification when no results returned
Integration test: run against known-good index, verify all queries pass
Integration test: run against empty index, verify all queries fail
risks: Test cases must be manually maintained and updated when content changes
Expected files are fragile — renames break tests
FTS5 tokenization differences between SQLite versions may affect results
Stopword list is English-only; CJK queries need different tokenization
do_not_do: Do not run validation against production index without a backup
Do not hardcode expected file paths without a maintenance plan
Do not skip the LIKE fallback — FTS5 alone may miss valid results
notes: Extracted from DataBase scripts/backend/validate_backend_search.py (188 lines). The pass/partial/fail classification and expected-files matching logic is backend-agnostic.
